summary.intent: User wanted to ensure Ollama and the contextplus MCP server auto-start with every Claude Code session to avoid manual setup.
summary.outcome: Confirmed Ollama auto-starts on macOS login via Homebrew service, and contextplus MCP server auto-starts when opening Claude Code sessions in the project due to .mcp.json configuration.

repo: Project uses contextplus MCP server configured in .mcp.json with nomic-embed-text embeddings and llama3.2 chat model
contextplus provides semantic search tools (get_context_tree, semantic_code_search, get_blast_radius) that depend on Ollama
workflow: MCP servers defined in .mcp.json auto-start when opening Claude Code sessions in that directory
Ollama can be managed as a Homebrew service (brew services start ollama) to auto-launch on macOS login
Launch agents are stored in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ and handle auto-start behavior on macOS

friction: Initial confusion about whether Ollama was running - 'ollama serve' failed because port was already in use
User unclear about which MCP server needed starting and whether it would persist across sessions
Ambiguity around 'login' terminology - user didn't immediately understand it meant macOS system login
